multiplechoiceresponse.label: Under ε-demographic parity with ε = 0.01, which condition must hold?

choicegroup.choice: The average risk score must differ by less than 0.01 across groups
The difference in the fraction of individuals labeled “high risk” across groups must be ≤ 0.01
The classifier must achieve at least 99% accuracy
False positive rates must differ by no more than 1%

div.p: Demographic parity is defined in terms of decision rates, not scores or errors. ε specifies the maximum allowable difference in the probability of receiving a positive outcome across groups. Accuracy and error rates are separate considerations and are not constrained by demographic parity. The constraint applies directly to predicted outcomes.
